preamble: The transformation of [ɛ] into [uo] involves backing and rounding, resulting in a centered or back diphthong. This is often part of a 'breaking' process where a monophthong develops a glide towards a different vowel height or backness.

examples.note: Backing and diphthongization into a centered-to-back glide.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
